title: The Cleanup of the Rebuild of Evangelion
display_title: The Cleanup of the Rebuild of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: The Cleanup of the Rebuild of Evangelion [NO SPOILERS]             Quote Post by EdZ » Tue Aug 25, 2009 10:22 am Had a go at cleaning up the camrip of 2.0. It's really just the original footage distorted to resemble the correct shape, with a whopping great Unsharp Mask filter plonked over the top (it'd kill any fine detail, but there isn't any in the first place). Took about 25 minutes in total to encode a 2 1/2 minute clip, so the whole thing would take a rather significant amount of time. Sample (Clockiel scene). SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Aug 25, 2009 1:31 pm I'd expect the DVD/BD release to be Spring next year. So the better question is: Is the impovement noticeable enough to justify not waiting? I didn't see the camrip so I'm waiting, and I'd suggest just waiting, too. Because the camrip doesn't look worth it and a fixing of the angle doesn't really help that much.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 25, 2009 2:11 pm I AM WAITING MY BUTT OFF.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Tue Aug 25, 2009 2:46 pm Nice effort EdZ. Now that the angle is corrected you can see the film a bit better. As well the contrast between light and dark seems to have improved noticeably. I would say that judging from that clip you posted it looks twice as good as the original cam-rip did. thanks. good work. By all accounts even after the clean-up this film still doesnt look dvd quality of course. But its a nice addition to have allowing us to see just a little bit more. Question though. Has your clean-up efforts improved the visibility for the dark Unit 05 and boniel battle scenes near the beginning of the film?

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Aug 25, 2009 3:10 pm Thanks Edz. I really appreciated the fruit of your efforts - feels like the first time I've truly appreciated that scene as well. However, I'm afraid it would suck way too many hours out of your life to do the whole movie...

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Tue Aug 25, 2009 3:15 pm Electric Sachiel wrote: Question though. Has your clean-up efforts improved the visibility for the dark Unit 05 and boniel battle scenes near the beginning of the film?

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Tue Aug 25, 2009 4:15 pm funny i did this myself using virtualdubmod.... dont see why yours is taking so long and giving you such big files. What i did with mine was convert it to avi cropped the screen so you only see the picture. no cinema/heads. feels a lot more immersion put a very slight smooth filter on to remove grain put a very slight brightness filter on at varying levels on differecnt scenes and finally put a flicker reduction filter on it all came out very well and was no more then 800mb in 16:9 ill upload it if any1 wants a copy

[]:             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Tue Aug 25, 2009 4:46 pm @skikes: I'd like to check it out if you could send a message my way.

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Tue Aug 25, 2009 4:47 pm skikes wrote: funny i did this myself using virtualdubmod.... dont see why yours is taking so long and giving you such big files.[...] cropped the screen so you only see the picture. no cinema/heads.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Tue Aug 25, 2009 8:44 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I AM WAITING MY BUTT OFF.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Tue Aug 25, 2009 11:19 pm Funny how that works out, I don't think there would be much different in terms of my idea of what happens in 2.0 cause I think I interpreted everything that occured in the RAW pretty well IMPALE

[]: The Cleanup of the Rebuild of Evangelion [NO SPOILERS]             Quote Post by EdZ » Tue Aug 25, 2009 10:22 am Had a go at cleaning up the camrip of 2.0. It's really just the original footage distorted to resemble the correct shape, with a whopping great Unsharp Mask filter plonked over the top (it'd kill any fine detail, but there isn't any in the first place). Took about 25 minutes in total to encode a 2 1/2 minute clip, so the whole thing would take a rather significant amount of time. Sample (Clockiel scene). SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Aug 25, 2009 1:31 pm I'd expect the DVD/BD release to be Spring next year. So the better question is: Is the impovement noticeable enough to justify not waiting? I didn't see the camrip so I'm waiting, and I'd suggest just waiting, too. Because the camrip doesn't look worth it and a fixing of the angle doesn't really help that much.

As well the contrast between light and dark seems to have improved noticeably. I would say that judging from that clip you posted it looks twice as good as the original cam-rip did. thanks. good work. By all accounts even after the clean-up this film still doesnt look dvd quality of course. But its a nice addition to have allowing us to see just a little bit more. Question though. Has your clean-up efforts improved the visibility for the dark Unit 05 and boniel battle scenes near the beginning of the film?
